name: Valle del Aliso Trail
name_original: Almorchón — circular por la Sierra de la Rinconada
description: Hike the difficult 11.6-mile Almorchón circular route through the Sierra de la Rinconada, featuring valley views and Roman baths.
region_intro: Hiking around Cabeza Del Buey offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra del Pedregoso, part of the Sierra Morena foothills. The region features significant elevations such as Tiros (957 meters) and Sierra de la Rinconada (844 meters), providing varied terrain. Hikers can experience Mediterranean landscapes and panoramic views of the vast La Serena reservoir. The area is characterized by mountain ranges, rocky shelters, and natural springs. Best hiking trails around Cabeza Del Buey The most popular hiking route is Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198), a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the La Serena reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer – round trip via the Castillo and the Embalse de la Serena, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This route includes views of the Castillo and the Embalse de la Serena. Local hikers also love the Cabeza del Bouy — circular by Ermita del Calvario and Sierra de la Vaca, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through mountain ranges, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Cabeza Del Buey is defined by mountain ranges, Mediterranean landscapes, and views of the La Serena reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Cabeza Del Buey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabeza Del Buey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Cabeza Del Buey offers a network of 18 hiking trails. These include 4 easy routes, 8 moderate paths, and 6 more difficult ascents, providing options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Hiking in Cabeza Del Buey takes you through diverse landscapes within the Sierra del Pedregoso, part of the Sierra Morena foothills. You'll encounter significant elevations like Tiros (957 meters) and Sierra de la Rinconada (844 meters), Mediterranean landscapes, and panoramic views of the vast La Serena reservoir. The area is also characterized by rocky shelters and natural springs.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural features along the trails in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural features and viewpoints. You can enjoy panoramic views of the La Serena reservoir, especially from trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991604893/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail-sl-ba-198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198)&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable spots include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt; itself, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cerro Masatrigo from Mirador Celeste de Galizuela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Yes, Cabeza Del Buey features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293785574/cabeza-del-bouy-circular-by-ermita-del-calvario-and-sierra-de-la-vaca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza del Bouy — circular by Ermita del Calvario and Sierra de la Vaca&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 14.7 km trail offering impressive views from the Ermita del Calvario. Another is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294250828/sierra-de-las-poyatas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Las Poyatas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 21.7 km.

Q: What historical sites or cultural points of interest can I find along the hiking trails?
A: Cabeza Del Buey is rich in history. Hikers can encounter vestiges of ancient civilizations, including Paleolithic remains, numerous cave paintings across the Sierras de Tiros, Rinconada, and Pedregoso, and the Cerroloboso dolmen. The Roman baths of La Nava are another unique point of interest. You might also see historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; from trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101692844/mirador-celeste-de-galizuela-from-puebla-de-alcocer-round-trip-via-the-castillo-and-the-embalse-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: While specific data on the best time to visit isn't provided, the region's Mediterranean climate suggests spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant hiking conditions, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat than summer. Winter can also be suitable, though some higher elevations might experience colder weather.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Yes, among the 18 trails, there are 4 routes classified as easy, which are generally suitable for families. These trails offer a gentler introduction to the region's natural beauty without demanding significant physical exertion.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: The trails in Cabeza Del Buey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the La Serena reservoir, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the La Serena reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The vast La Serena reservoir is a prominent feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991604893/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail-sl-ba-198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically noted for its panoramic views of the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101692844/mirador-celeste-de-galizuela-from-puebla-de-alcocer-round-trip-via-the-castillo-and-the-embalse-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; also includes views of the Embalse de la Serena.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on the hiking trails in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: The terrain in Cabeza Del Buey is characterized by mountain ranges, including the Sierra del Pedregoso, and Mediterranean landscapes. You can expect paths that range from gentle slopes to more challenging ascents, often featuring rocky sections and natural springs, reflecting the region's diverse topography.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by ancient sites or historical ruins?
A: Yes, several trails offer glimpses into the region's ancient past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294175776/penon-del-pez-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through Mediterranean landscapes and ancient sites, including views of Capilla Castle. The area is also known for its cave paintings and the Cerroloboso dolmen, which can be explored on various routes.
tips: The Almorchón train station has the Puertollano-Zafra regional line. A great place to start your hiking adventure in this area.
Ruins of settlements from the Roman era and their love for the well-being provided by the hot thermal waters at the foot of the mountain of Puerto de la Nava.

The path reaches the port of La Nava and the view of the valley opens up. The port is located between the peaks of the Sierra de la Osa and the Sierra de la Vaca, descending towards Almorchón or Cabeza del Buey.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular hike is approximately 18.7 km long and typically takes around 5 hours and 9 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is officially rated as difficult. It is best suited for experienced hikers with good physical fitness due to its length and elevation changes. Beginners may find it too strenuous.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead and parking for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, and a common starting point is near the Almorchón Station. Specific parking information for this exact trailhead is not detailed, but generally, you can find parking in the vicinity of such stations or nearby villages.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Given that a key highlight is Almorchón Station, it suggests that train services might be an option to reach the starting point. It's advisable to check local train schedules for services to Almorchón.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you can discover several notable spots. Highlights include the historic Almorchón Station, the ancient Roman Baths of Puerto de la Nava, and stunning Valley views from Puerto de la Nava.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific information for this trail is limited, generally for difficult hikes in regions like Sierra de la Rinconada, spring and autumn are ideal. The temperatures are milder, making the long distance and elevation gain more manageable than in the heat of summer.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog regulations for this particular trail is not available. However, in many natural areas in Spain, dogs are permitted on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or regulations before heading out with your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is located in La Serena. Generally, hiking trails in natural areas like Sierra de la Rinconada do not require specific permits or entrance fees for access. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or protected area rules that might apply.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult hike?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and length, you should come prepared. Essential gear includes sturdy hiking boots, plenty of water (at least 2-3 liters), snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), a first-aid kit, and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can also be very helpful on varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Almorchón circular route?
faqs.answer: As a difficult hike in the Sierra de la Rinconada, you can expect varied and challenging terrain. This likely includes rocky paths, uneven surfaces, and significant ascents and descents. Hikers should be prepared for a demanding walk.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: Information about specific facilities directly on this circular route is not available. Given its difficult rating and natural setting, it's safest to assume there are no restrooms or cafes directly on the trail. Plan to bring all necessary supplies and use facilities before or after your hike, possibly near Almorchón Station or in nearby towns.
